title: Systems and methods for data distribution using a publication subscriber model with a federation of trusted data distribution networks
abstract: Federation of trusted data distribution systems is accomplished by treating an entire data distribution network as either a publisher or subscriber to a feed in another data distribution network. A first data feed is created in a first data feed management subsystem associated with a first data distribution network. A second data feed related to the first data feed is created in a second data feed management subsystem associated with a second data distribution network. A first data access policy is associated with the second data feed and a publisher for the second data feed is created in the second data distribution network. The identity and authentication of a second subscriber to the second data feed in the second data distribution network is managed by referencing the first data access policy
claims:
Claims What is Claimed: 1. A method comprising: creating a first data feed in a first data feed management subsystem associated with a first data distribution network; creating a second data feed related to the first data feed in a second data feed management subsystem associated with a second data distribution network; associating a first data access policy with the second data feed; creating a publisher for the second data feed in the second data distribution network wherein the publisher is accessible to a plurality of subscribers in the second data distribution network; and managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber to the second data feed in the second data distribution network by referencing the first data access policy. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein creating a publisher for the second data feed comprises: creating a first subscriber to the first data feed in the first data feed management subsystem; routing the first subscriber to the second data distribution network; and designating the first subscriber as a publisher for the second data feed. 3. The method of claim 1 further comprising receiving a request for access to the second data feed from the second subscriber in the second data distribution network. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber comprises determining if access to the second data feed by the second subscriber is allowed based on the first data access policy combined with existing data access policies in the second data distribution network. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein associating a first data access policy comprises accessing a policy engine to determine what actions an individual, a publisher, or a subscriber, is allowed to take with respect to the second data feed management subsystem. 6. The method of claim 1 wherein the second data feed is identical to the first data feed. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein associating a first data access policy with the second data feed comprises accessing a policy engine that determines what actions an individual, a publisher, or a subscriber, is allowed to take with respect to the first data feed management subsystem. 8. A system comprising: a first data distribution network comprising: a first data movement subsystem; a first identity management and authentication subsystem; a first policy engine; and a first data feed management subsystem; a second data distribution network comprising: a second data movement subsystem; a second identity management and authentication subsystem; a second policy engine; and a second data feed management subsystem; a processor that executes instructions to perform operations, the operations comprising: creating a first data feed in a first data feed management subsystem associated with a first data distribution network; creating a second data feed related to the first data feed in a second data feed management subsystem associated with a second data distribution network; associating a first data access policy with the second data feed; creating a publisher for the second data feed in the second data distribution network, wherein the publisher is accessible to a plurality of subscribers in the second data distribution network; and managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber to the second data feed in the second data distribution network by referencing the first data access policy. 9. The system of claim 8 wherein creating a publisher for the second data feed comprises: creating a first subscriber to the first data feed in the first data feed management subsystem; routing the first subscriber to the second data distribution network; and 

 designating the first subscriber as a publisher for the second data feed. 10. The system of claim 8 wherein the operations executed by instructions from the processor further comprises receiving a request for access to the second data feed from the second subscriber in the second data distribution network. 1 1. The system of claim 8 wherein managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber comprises determining if access to the second data feed by the second subscriber is allowed based on the first data access policy combined with existing data access policies in the second data distribution network. 12. The system of claim 8 wherein associating a first data access policy comprises accessing a policy engine to determine what actions an individual, a publisher, or a subscriber, is allowed to take with respect to the second data feed management subsystem. 13. The system of claim 8 wherein the processor executes instructions to perform operations comprising authenticating the first subscriber. 14. The system of claim 8 wherein the second data feed is identical to the first data feed. 15. A computer-readable device comprising instructions, which, when loaded and executed by a processor, cause the processor to perform operations, the operations comprising: creating a first data feed in a first data feed management subsystem associated with a first data distribution network; creating a second data feed related to the first data feed in a second data feed management subsystem associated with a second data distribution network; associating a first data access policy with the second data feed; creating a publisher for the second data feed in the second data distribution network wherein the publisher is accessible to a plurality of subscribers in the second data distribution network; and managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber to the second data feed in the second data distribution network by referencing the first data access policy. 16. The computer-readable device of claim 15 wherein creating a publisher for the second data feed comprises: creating a first subscriber to the first data feed in the first data feed management subsystem; routing the first subscriber to the second data distribution network; and designating the first subscriber as a publisher for the second data feed. 17. The computer-readable device of claim 15 comprising instructions, which when loaded and executed by the processor, cause the processor to perform operations further comprising receiving a request for access to the second data feed from the second subscriber in the second data distribution network. 18. The computer-readable device of claim 15 wherein managing identity and authentication of a second subscriber comprises determining if access to the second data feed by the second subscriber is allowed based on the first data access policy combined with existing data access policies in the second data distribution network. 19. The computer-readable device of claim 15 wherein associating a first data access policy comprises accessing a policy engine to determine what actions an individual, a publisher, or a subscriber, is allowed to take with respect to the second data feed management subsystem. 20. The computer-readable device of claim 15 comprising instructions, which, when loaded and executed by a processor, cause the processor to perform operations, the operations further comprising authenticating the first subscriber.
